challenge_text: Without any admin command, enumerate topics, count subscribers per topic, read drop counters, and list pending deferred messages using only ls and cat. SUCCESS: ls topics/, ls topics/$T/sub_*.fifo | wc -l, cat topics/$T/drops_*, ls deferred/ all produce sensible output; no bespoke CLI needed.

friction_points.description: Reproducing a visible drop as a pure user is non-obvious. My first three attempts to wedge a subscriber (piping the socat reader to /dev/null, or short payloads) produced zero drops even under an 8000-msg flood (healthy sub silently received only 4005/8000), because a wedged peer backpressures the single-threaded publisher delivery loop rather than overflowing the ring. The drops counter only ticked (drops_33 -> 352) once I matched the official test's exact recipe: SHELLMUX_WRITE_TIMEOUT=0.002 on serve, ~400B payloads, AND the wedged socat's OUTPUT side also left unread via '| ( exec sleep 60 )'. None of this is documented for an operator who wants to provoke/observe a drop; DEMO Beat 4 shows how to READ drops_* but not how to make one appear outside running flood_wedged.sh.
friction_points.workaround: Run tests/flood_wedged.sh, or replicate its wedge recipe (unread socat output + small WRITE_TIMEOUT + padded payloads).

friction_points.description: The drops file is named drops_<pid> (e.g. drops_33) and so is the FIFO sub_<pid>.fifo, but there is no operator-facing way to map a pid back to which subscriber/connection it was after that subscriber is gone. For a healthy topic you cannot tell which historical sub dropped; the counter is the only artifact and it persists with a bare numeric pid. Minor: an operator doing forensics ('which client lost messages?') has only the pid, no topic-local label.
friction_points.workaround: Correlate the pid with broker.log / ps at the time of the incident.

wished_for: A documented one-liner to deliberately wedge a sub and watch drops_* tick (operator drill).
An optional aggregate readout (e.g. a total drops view) so an operator doesn't have to glob cat across many drops_<pid> files on a busy topic.
what_worked_well: ls topics/ listed exactly the topics created (weather, alerts) with zero ceremony.
ls topics/$T/sub_*.fifo | wc -l gave an honest live subscriber count (2 on weather, 1 on alerts); after killing one sub it correctly dropped to 1 (forget-on-death via filesystem).

cat topics/$T/drops_* with 2>/dev/null || echo 'no drops' behaves exactly as DEMO predicts: ENOENT (no file) on a healthy topic, and a sensible integer (352) once the lazily-created drops_33 appears on first drop.
ls deferred/ showed the pending deadline as a single <run_at_ms>.<seq> filename (1782185538676.1423812427) — readable, sortable, exactly what a MIN(run_at) scheduler needs.
Entire introspection done with ls/cat only; no bespoke admin CLI required. Challenge fully satisfied.
Zero raw bash errors in broker.log across every malformed-input probe.
comparison_to_expectations: Round-001 was 8/10. The filesystem-as-API introspection contract held up cleanly and every round-001 regression my challenge could reach is RESOLVED with no regressions. The lazy drops_<pid> creation, traversal rejection, far-future-deadline rejection, malformed --at/--delay rejection (nonzero rc, no raw bash errors), --help exit 0, and flood_wedged cold-start stability (3/3 runs, no hang) all verified live. Bumped to 9: docs now predict observed behavior precisely. Held back from 10 by the discoverability gap that an operator can't easily provoke a drop without copying the test's internal wedge recipe.
free_text: Pure black-box session in the pinned container. Verified the full challenge: ls topics/ -> {alerts,weather}; ls topics/$T/sub_*.fifo|wc -l -> correct counts that decrement on subscriber death; cat topics/$T/drops_* -> 'no drops' (ENOENT) healthy then 352 after a real ring overflow (drops_33 created lazily on first drop, exactly as DEMO line 81/87 claims); ls deferred/ -> a single <run_at>.<seq> file from pub --delay 60. No admin command needed anywhere. Honesty check: DEMO/README now PREDICT what I observed (lazy drops file, RAM-bound ceiling wording, at-most-once-modulo-crash, newline-delimited NUL-free text contract, hostile-input rejection at the boundary). The only friction is that the drop counter is hard to exercise as an operator without the test's exact wedge recipe, and the drops file carries only a bare pid.

claim_audit.claim: A drops_<pid> file is created lazily on the first dropped record, so a healthy topic has none
claim_audit.evidence: cat topics/t/drops_* returned ENOENT (no drops) before flood; after a real ring overflow a file 'drops_33' appeared containing '352'.

claim_audit.claim: State IS the filesystem; no admin protocol
claim_audit.evidence: Enumerated topics, sub counts, drop counters, and pending deferred messages entirely with ls/cat; no bespoke CLI invoked.

claim_audit.claim: ls deferred/ shows the pending deadline as <run_at_ms>.<seq>
Topic names must be [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ with no leading dot; traversal rejected, nothing created outside state dir
claim_audit.evidence: pub '../../../tmp/PWNED' returned rc=1 'bad topic name'; /tmp/PWNED did not exist; topics/ stayed empty.

claim_audit.claim: --at/--delay must be a non-negative integer within SHELLMUX_MAX_DEFER_S or publish returns nonzero
claim_audit.evidence: --at xyz rc=1, --delay 1.5 rc=1, --at 99999999999 rc=1; deferred/ stayed empty; no raw bash errors in broker.log.

proof_assessment.notes: Not in scope for the introspection challenge; I did not run the chaos gate this session. The deferred-message introspection path (ls deferred/) was exercised and behaved correctly, but the missed/dup proof itself was out of scope.

regression_results.evidence: pub t1 --at xyz -> 'shellmux: bad --at ...' rc=1; pub t1 --delay 1.5 -> 'shellmux: bad --delay ...' rc=1; broker.log grep for 'unbound variable|value too great|invalid arithmetic|syntax error' returned nothing.
pub t1 --at 99999999999 -> rc=1 'bad --at ... within SHELLMUX_MAX_DEFER_S'; ls deferred/ stayed empty afterward.
pub '../../../tmp/PWNED' -> rc=1 'bad topic name'; /tmp/PWNED did not exist; topics/ empty. No mkdir outside state dir.
bash src/shellmux sub --help rc=0; pub --help rc=0; no '$2: unbound variable' crash.
tests/flood_wedged.sh run 3x back-to-back: all pass=4 fail=0, max_descendants 19/19/20, drops 360/378/396, no cold-start hang (each well under the 130s cap).
cat topics/$T/drops_* on healthy topic returns ENOENT, the '2>/dev/null || echo no drops' idiom from DEMO works; drops_33 appears only after a real first drop.

README.md:42-49 and DEMO.md:123-127 now state the honest contract: one newline-delimited NUL-free TEXT line, NUL stripped, payload truncated at first newline, unterminated not delivered. Doc matches the line-oriented behavior I observed (a sub received whole lines only).
DEMO.md:128-133 and README.md:46-49 name 'at-most-once-modulo-crash' explicitly for overdue-deferred-on-reboot.
DEMO.md:105-109 now states the subscriber ceiling is RAM-bound (~2.4MB/sub), not fd/process-bound.
